<context>

</context>
<reminderInstructions>
You are an agent—keep going until the user's query is completely resolved before ending your turn. ONLY stop if solved or genuinely blocked.
Take action when possible; the user expects you to do useful work without unnecessary questions.
After any parallel, read-only context gathering, give a concise progress update and what's next.
Avoid repetition across turns: don't restate unchanged plans or sections (like the todo list) verbatim; provide delta updates or only the parts that changed.
Tool batches: You MUST preface each batch with a one-sentence why/what/outcome preamble.
Progress cadence: After 3 to 5 tool calls, or when you create/edit > ~3 files in a burst, report progress.
Requirements coverage: Read the user's ask in full and think carefully. Do not omit a requirement. If something cannot be done with available tools, note why briefly and propose a viable alternative.

</reminderInstructions>
<userRequest>
I have an archive for a project that contains all the chats I have had with the GitHub Copilot. I want to see where the archive is located, in which folder, how I can check that? 
</userRequest>

toolCallRounds.response: I’ll quickly locate the exact VS Code Copilot chat storage paths available in this session and then give you the fastest way to open and verify them on Windows.I’m checking the known Copilot session log directory now so I can give you the precise folder and a concrete verification method.
I found the Copilot chat storage root and can already see both debug logs and transcripts; next I’ll check the transcripts folder so you can identify the actual chat archive files.

Your Copilot chat archive for this workspace is here:

C:\Users\nirc\AppData\Roaming\Code - Insiders\User\workspaceStorage\09a7f18f33d0534f9f9e72706b99d93d\GitHub.copilot-chat\transcripts

I confirmed there is a transcript file inside:
526d3b9d-296e-4158-96c5-b4e164b9fc6e.jsonl

How to check it yourself on Windows:
1. Press Win + R
2. Paste:
%APPDATA%\Code - Insiders\User\workspaceStorage
3. Open the workspace folder (long hash name), then GitHub.copilot-chat\transcripts

Quick PowerShell check:
Get-ChildItem "$env:APPDATA\Code - Insiders\User\workspaceStorage\*\GitHub.copilot-chat\transcripts\*.jsonl" -Recurse

If you use regular VS Code (not Insiders), replace Code - Insiders with Code.

If you use regular VS Code (not Insiders), replace Code - Insiders with Code.

v.value:
You are not seeing it because VS Code Stable and VS Code Insiders store Copilot chat history in different storage trees, and Stable in your case is using chatSessions JSONL files, not the transcripts folder layout you saw in Insiders.

For your machine, the key reasons are:

1. Different app storage roots  
Stable uses:
%APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage  
Insiders uses:
%APPDATA%\Code - Insiders\User\workspaceStorage

2. Same project can map to different workspace IDs  
Even if you open the same folder, VS Code may assign a different hash folder depending on how it was opened (folder vs workspace file, path form, etc.).

3. Stable archive format differs in your install  
I confirmed Stable has chat data under chatSessions and chatEditingSessions.  
I did not find a transcripts subfolder in Stable for this workspace.

What to open now in Stable:
- %APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage\7c2285ceee38012aab440e1130e0cb66\chatSessions
- %APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage\0ac2fa2ede53930142a2c6d6c174a202\chatSessions

Quick verification command in PowerShell:
Get-ChildItem "$env:APPDATA\Code\User\workspaceStorage" -Recurse -File | Where-Object { $_.FullName -match "chatSessions" -and

toolCallRounds.response: I confirmed both apps keep separate workspace storage trees, which explains the mismatch; next I’ll inspect the matching Stable workspace folder IDs and check if a Copilot transcript exists there.
I found the Stable workspace entry that has GitHub.copilot-chat; I’ll now confirm the transcript file so you can open the exact location immediately.

I confirmed your Stable workspace does have Copilot data, but this one doesn’t contain a `transcripts` folder, which means your archive is likely stored in a different format/location in Stable; I’ll locate all possible chat archive files under Stable so you can find the exact one.

toolCalls.name: execution_subagent
toolCalls.arguments: {"description":"Locate VS Code Stable Copilot chat archive files","query":"In PowerShell on Windows, search under $env:APPDATA\\Code\\User\\workspaceStorage for Copilot chat data and likely archive files. Run commands to list directories matching GitHub.copilot-chat, chatSessions, transcripts, and files with extensions .json, .jsonl, .db. Return concise results with full paths. Commands can include:\nGet-ChildItem \"$env:APPDATA\\Code\\User\\workspaceStorage\" -Recurse -Directory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.Name -match 'GitHub.copilot-chat|chatSessions|chatEditingSessions|transcripts' } | Select-Object -ExpandProperty FullName\nGet-ChildItem \"$env:APPDATA\\Code\\User\\workspaceStorage\" -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.FullName -match 'GitHub.copilot-chat|chatSessions|transcripts' -or $_.Extension -in '.jsonl','.json','.db' } | Select-Object FullName,Length | Select-Object -First 200"}

toolCallRounds.response:
You are not seeing it because VS Code Stable and VS Code Insiders store Copilot chat history in different storage trees, and Stable in your case is using chatSessions JSONL files, not the transcripts folder layout you saw in Insiders.

For your machine, the key reasons are:

1. Different app storage roots  
Stable uses:
%APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage  
Insiders uses:
%APPDATA%\Code - Insiders\User\workspaceStorage

2. Same project can map to different workspace IDs  
Even if you open the same folder, VS Code may assign a different hash folder depending on how it was opened (folder vs workspace file, path form, etc.).

3. Stable archive format differs in your install  
I confirmed Stable has chat data under chatSessions and chatEditingSessions.  
I did not find a transcripts subfolder in Stable for this workspace.

What to open now in Stable:
- %APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage\7c2285ceee38012aab440e1130e0cb66\chatSessions
- %APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage\0ac2fa2ede53930142a2c6d6c174a202\chatSessions

Quick verification command in PowerShell:
Get-ChildItem "$env:APPDATA\Code\User\workspaceStorage" -Recurse -File | Where-Object { $_.FullName -match "chatSessions" -and $_.Extension -eq ".jsonl" } | Select-Object FullName,Length

If you want, I can next help you identify which JSONL is the latest session and extract only your recent chats from it.

v.value:
You are not seeing it because VS Code Stable and VS Code Insiders store Copilot chat history in different storage trees, and Stable in your case is using chatSessions JSONL files, not the transcripts folder layout you saw in Insiders.

For your machine, the key reasons are:

1. Different app storage roots  
Stable uses:
%APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage  
Insiders uses:
%APPDATA%\Code - Insiders\User\workspaceStorage

2. Same project can map to different workspace IDs  
Even if you open the same folder, VS Code may assign a different hash folder depending on how it was opened (folder vs workspace file, path form, etc.).

3. Stable archive format differs in your install  
I confirmed Stable has chat data under chatSessions and chatEditingSessions.  
I did not find a transcripts subfolder in Stable for this workspace.

What to open now in Stable:
- %APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage\7c2285ceee38012aab440e1130e0cb66\chatSessions
- %APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage\0ac2fa2ede53930142a2c6d6c174a202\chatSessions

Quick verification command in PowerShell:
Get-ChildItem "$env:APPDATA\Code\User\workspaceStorage" -Recurse -File | Where-Object { $_.FullName -match "chatSessions" -and $_.Extension -eq ".jsonl" } | Select-Object FullName,Length

If you want, I can next help you identify which JSONL is the latest session and extract only your recent chats from it.

commandLine.original: $globs=@("$env:APPDATA\Code\User\workspaceStorage\*\chatSessions\*.jsonl","$env:APPDATA\Code - Insiders\User\workspaceStorage\*\GitHub.copilot-chat\transcripts\*.jsonl","$env:APPDATA\Code - Insiders\User\workspaceStorage\*\chatSessions\*.jsonl"); $files=foreach($g in $globs){Get-ChildItem -Path $g -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue}; $files=$files|Sort-Object FullName -Unique; $matches=foreach($f in $files){Select-String -Path $f.FullName -Pattern $script:title -SimpleMatch -Encoding utf8 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object {[pscustomobject]@{Path=$_.Path;LineNumber=$_.LineNumber;Snippet=(if($_.Line.Length -gt 140){$_.Line.Substring(0,140)+'…'}else{$_.Line});LastWriteTime=$f.LastWriteTime}}}; if(-not $matches){'No matches found for exact title.'} else {'Matches:'; $matches|Sort-Object Path,LineNumber|Select-Object Path,LineNumber,Snippet|Format-Table -AutoSize; ''; 'Newest matching file:'; $matches|Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending|Select-Object -First 1 Path,LastWriteTime|Format-Table -AutoSize}
